# Front Shared Inbox Platform

Front is a shared inbox platform targeting teams that need to manage customer communication across multiple channels (email, SMS, social, live chat) with advanced collaboration features. It is particularly strong for customer success and account management teams that work alongside support.

## Key findings

**Multi-channel inbox:** Front aggregates email, SMS, WhatsApp, Twitter DMs, live chat, and custom API sources into a single inbox. This breadth makes it the most versatile multi-channel platform of the five reviewed; Intercom's Messenger is closest but weaker outside its own chat widget.

**Collaboration features:** Front's collaboration is strongest-in-class: internal comments (not visible to customer), @mention teammates, shared drafts, conflict detection (prevents double-replies), and rules-based auto-assignment. These features are built for teams of 5-50 support agents working simultaneously on high-volume inboxes.

**SLA and reporting:** Front's SLA policy engine is the most sophisticated of the five tools: SLAs scoped per inbox, per conversation attribute, per customer tier. Breach escalation rules send alerts to team leads and can automatically reassign conversations. Reports include CSAT trend, first-response time, resolution time, agent performance — all on the built-in analytics dashboard.

**API quality:** Front's REST API is well-documented and supports webhook subscriptions for all conversation events. Not GraphQL like Plain, but comprehensive enough for custom integrations. Front also has a Marketplace of 50+ integrations.

**Pricing:** Front's pricing is seat-based:
- Starter: $19/seat/month (2 inboxes, basic features)
- Growth: $59/seat/month (unlimited inboxes, rules, SLA, API)
- Scale: $99/seat/month (advanced reporting, Salesforce, enterprise SSO)

The Starter tier is notably cheap, making Front accessible for early-stage teams that need multi-channel. At > 5 agents, the Growth tier becomes necessary.

**AI features (Front AI):** Front AI includes AI Compose (draft a reply based on conversation context), AI Summarize, and AI Suggest (surface relevant knowledge base articles). These are copilot features; no autonomous resolution equivalent to Fin.

**Notion integration:** Front has a Notion integration in its Marketplace — agents can search Notion pages from within a conversation and paste links. This is the most complete Notion integration of the five tools reviewed.

## Key takeaways

- Front is the right tool for teams managing multi-channel customer communication (email + SMS + social) with 5-50 agents.
- Its SLA reporting and escalation rules are the most sophisticated of the five tools; recommend Front when management needs SLA reporting by customer tier or team.
- Front AI is copilot-level only; combine with Intercom for autonomous deflection if needed.
- The Notion integration is the most complete of the five tools — relevant for teams using Notion as their internal knowledge base.
- Front's Starter plan ($19/seat) makes it accessible for early-stage multi-channel teams; upgrade to Growth for SLA and API access.
